title: Classic Beef Wellington
description: A show-stopping centerpiece featuring tender beef filet wrapped in savory mushroom duxelles and golden puff pastry.
instructions: Sear the beef filets in a hot pan until browned on all sides, then let cool.
Finely chop mushrooms and cook down with thyme and sherry until dry (duxelles).
Spread mustard over the cooled beef, layer with prosciutto and mushroom mixture.
Wrap in puff pastry, seal edges, and egg wash the top.
Bake at 200°C (400°F) for 25-30 minutes until golden brown.

title: Lemon Herb Salmon
description: Fresh, healthy, and bursting with citrus flavor. This salmon is baked to perfection on a bed of asparagus.
instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F). Place asparagus on a baking sheet.
Mix olive oil, lemon juice, minced garlic, and chopped dill in a small bowl.
Place salmon on top of asparagus and drizzle with the lemon mixture.
Bake for 12-15 minutes until salmon flakes easily with a fork.

title: Traditional Tiramisu
description: The quintessential Italian dessert. Layers of coffee-soaked ladyfingers and rich mascarpone cream.
instructions: Whisk egg yolks and sugar until thick and pale. Fold in the mascarpone cheese.
Dip ladyfingers quickly into the espresso and layer them in a dish.
Spread half the mascarpone mixture, repeat layers, and finish with cocoa dusting.
Refrigerate for at least 4 hours to set.

title: Spicy Thai Basil Chicken
description: A quick, fiery, and aromatic dish perfect for a weeknight dinner with an authentic street-food flavor.
instructions: Stir-fry garlic and ginger in oil until fragrant.
Add chicken and cook until browned.
Add chilies, fish sauce, and soy sauce. Toss to coat.
Finish with a generous handful of fresh basil and serve immediately over rice.

title: Wild Mushroom Risotto
description: Creamy, earthy, and comforting. A vegetarian masterpiece made with a mix of wild mushrooms and arborio rice.
instructions: Sauté mushrooms in butter until golden. Remove and set aside.
Toast rice in the same pan, then deglaze with wine.
Add warm stock one ladle at a time, stirring constantly until absorbed.
Stir in mushrooms, parmesan, and finish with truffle oil.

title: French Onion Soup
description: A rich, comforting soup topped with toasted baguette and melted Gruyère cheese.
instructions: Caramelize onions slowly in butter and oil for 30-40 minutes.
Add wine and cook for 2 minutes, then add broth, thyme, and bay leaf. Simmer.
Ladle soup into oven-safe bowls, top with baguette slices and lots of cheese.
Broil until cheese is bubbly and golden brown.

title: Chocolate Lava Cake
description: Decadent individual chocolate cakes with a molten center. The ultimate chocolate lover's dessert.
instructions: Melt chocolate and butter together. Whisk eggs and sugar until pale.
Fold chocolate mix into eggs, then sift in flour and vanilla.
Pour into greased ramekins and bake at 200°C for 10-12 minutes.
Center should be soft. Let cool slightly before unmolding.
